q: STAR weighting for FDE — biggest section?
a: Action ~60% of the story. Be specific about both technical and interpersonal moves you made.
(1) Production fix under pressure. (2) Pushing back on customer. (3) Deployment failure ownership. (4) Explaining a technical limit. (5) Decision under incomplete info.
(1) AGI focus. (2) Intensity. (3) Scale. (4) Make something people love. Read the Charter; have a specific reason for the role.
Real engagement with safety + RSP + Constitutional AI. Ethical-dilemma scenarios common. Generic "I do the right thing" fails.

q: "Tell me about a time you disagreed with your manager" — what most candidates skip?
a: The COMMIT half: executing fully on the team\'s decision, even when you lost. Often stronger when you were partly wrong + did your share.

q: "Tell me about mentoring someone" — what makes it strong?
a: Specific person + specific gap + your approach + measurable outcome (promotion, project shipped, observable behavior change).
Logs (why did this fail), Metrics (is something wrong NOW), Traces (where in the call graph). OpenTelemetry unifies emission.
Vendor-neutral instrumentation library emitting logs/metrics/traces. Pluggable backends (Datadog, Jaeger, Honeycomb, etc.).
Metrics (what\'s wrong, narrow service) → Traces (find the slow span) → Logs (read the exception). Logs-first is junior.
Customer holds encryption key in their KMS. They can revoke = your service can\'t decrypt their data. Strong enterprise trust boundary.
Don\'t touch raw card data. Use Stripe/Adyen tokenization. PCI scope shrinks dramatically; compliance burden much smaller.
Data Processing Agreement. GDPR-required contract specifying how you handle customer\'s personal data. Sub-processors, residency, breach windows.

q: SAML SSO intermittent failure — top cause?
a: Clock skew. Assertions arrive with timestamps outside tolerance window. Allow 60s tolerance on NotBefore/NotOnOrAfter.
REST API for user/group provisioning. Customer\'s IdP automatically creates/updates/disables users in your system. Enterprise required.
Helm chart in their cluster, with all images mirrored into their internal container registry. No outbound to your servers.
Blue-green: two parallel envs, atomic cutover via LB flip. Canary: gradual % traffic shift with metric gates. Canary for risk; B/G for atomic rollback.
ACME protocol (Let\'s Encrypt) + cert-manager in K8s. Manual rotation fails when certs expire on weekends + incidents.
Spinnaker, Argo Rollouts. Watch metrics for N min post-deploy; if error rate / latency spikes, auto-revert. Standard for high-traffic services.
Dual-credential window: add new alongside old, roll services, remove old. Zero-downtime credential rotation.
Sidecars handle mTLS + retries + circuit-breaking + observability without code changes. Useful at scale; complex to operate.
8-16 week compliance review by hospital security teams. Plan for it. Single-tenant in customer VPC is often required (HIPAA).
Air-gapped on-prem deploys + FedRAMP High / IL5 + cleared engineers. Procurement cycle is months-years.
Double-entry + append-only (never UPDATE/DELETE) + idempotent writes. Adjustments via offsetting entries. Audit trail is sacred.
Long-running inference (minutes). Need async job queue + WS/polling progress + content moderation pre+post-gen + cancellation tokens.
Turn-taking + interruption handling. Latency is necessary but not sufficient for "feels human."

q: PLG vs SLG growth motion — which needs FDE?
a: SLG (sales-led, enterprise customers). FDE is the customer-engineering arm. PLG companies usually don\'t need traditional FDE.
Greedy by rating starves new entrants. ML rank with diversity injection (5-10% to newer suppliers) keeps supply healthy.
Re-reading concepts you already know. Testing yourself + practicing under pressure is higher-leverage per hour.

q: Kill criterion for stopping prep on a topic?
a: Can you teach it aloud + answer 3 follow-ups in 5 min? Yes → move on. Deeper study has diminishing returns past that.
~2× follow-through vs goal-setting alone (Gollwitzer). "When X, then Y" plans turn intentions into automatic triggers.

q: Concurrent process portfolio target during search?
a: 3-5 concurrent processes. Gives competing-offer leverage + risk diversification + faster timeline.
Manager quality. Worth weighting heavily in offer comparison even at ±$40k diff.
"Want to focus on whether the role is a fit. Market for FDE at your size is roughly $X-$Y total comp. Happy to be specific once aligned."
[Verb] [system you owned] [specific mechanism] [quantified outcome]. Recruiters skim 6-20 sec — verb + number carry signal.
When you have 70% of the info you need, decide. Waiting for 90% usually costs more than acting on 70%. Especially for reversible decisions.
At offer time AND every annual review. Refresh grants are negotiable, not automatic. Most engineers leave money on the table.
(1) Implementation intention (when-then). (2) 2-minute rule (start cost <30s). (3) Habit stacking (after coffee → study). (4) Environment design (pinned tab).
30-min "feel bad" block → resume next day. Don\'t check email obsessively (fix time). Maintain 3-5 concurrent processes so rejections are data, not verdicts.
Acknowledge impact ("I hear you") → Diagnose visibly ("checking X now, can you tell me Y?") → Own resolution timeline ("status by EOD, here\'s plan").
Acknowledge + run two tracks: visible (show recorded version) + backend (debug). Never blame customer\'s env in the moment. RCA within 24h.

q: Customer demands 6 weeks in 3 days — move?
a: Decompose to the 20% that unblocks them + document tradeoffs + get the REAL deadline (often softer than stated).

q: Same problem recurs 2 hours after fix — response?
a: Own as Sev1 + commit specific timeline + status updates every 4 hours. Defending the prior fix kills trust further.
Shipped this week · 🚧 In flight · ⚠️ Risks/blockers · 📅 Next week. 5 bullets read better than 3 paragraphs.

q: Customer\'s IT team blocks integration — move?
a: Structured security review proposal (SOC2 docs + architecture + sub-processor list). Bypassing them = political damage.

q: A customer-side champion is leaving — what next?
a: Get reintroduced to replacement + re-confirm scope/timeline with new owner. Champion loss = retention risk.

q: Explaining stochastic AI to a CFO?
a: Weather-forecast analogy + show confidence range + 95th-percentile worst case + leading indicators. Quant always wins with CFOs.
